hook.text: An unguarded machine pulled a worker's glove—and hand—into its gears in a fraction of a second.

buildup.text: Machines with moving parts—gears, belts, blades—create crush, shear, and entanglement hazards.

discovery.text: Guards physically prevent body parts from reaching danger zones around machines.

twist.text: Workers often remove guards to speed up production—then get injured doing what they always did.

climax.text: Interlocked guards that shut down the machine when opened eliminate the temptation to bypass.

punchline.text: The guard doesn't slow you down. It keeps your hands attached.

quiz.question: What type of guard shuts down a machine when opened?
